source.idea: The last human gardener on Mars receives seeds labelled with the names of people who have not been born yet.

B.narration:
In the vast emptiness of Mars, a single biodome stood as a testament to humanity's hope. Inside, Elara tended to her garden, the last of its kind.
Each seed I plant carries a story. They are all I have left of Earth, and the future they promise.
Elara carefully inspected a new batch of seeds she had received. They were labeled with names she had never heard before.
Who are these people? Why are their names on these seeds? It feels like a message from the past, or perhaps a glimpse into the future.
As she pondered, a sudden alarm blared through the biodome, shaking her from her thoughts. Something was wrong outside.
Elara rushed to the control panel, her heart racing. The readings showed a rapid increase in radiation levels. The dome was compromised!
I can't let this garden die! It’s all that remains of our legacy. I have to protect it at all costs!
Elara quickly gathered supplies and prepared to reinforce the biodome. She knew failure was not an option; the future depended on her.
As she worked, the alarms intensified. The radiation levels were rising dangerously, threatening everything she loved.
Please, let me save you. I can’t let these seeds die with me. You have to grow, you have to thrive!
With a final push, Elara secured the last panel. The dome hummed with life, the plants glowing defiantly against the encroaching darkness.
I will not let this garden fade. I will nurture every seed, every name, into a new world!
As the alarms faded, Elara took a deep breath. The garden was safe, for now. She looked at the seeds, her heart filled with hope.
Each of you carries a legacy. I will plant you all, and one day, you will bloom into the future we dreamed of.
Elara began to plant the seeds, each one a promise of life. The last gardener of Mars would not let hope die.
In the heart of the Martian desert, a new garden began to grow, a symbol of resilience and the unyielding spirit of humanity.

character_states.continuity_notes: Keep appearance and screen side stable within sc1

characters.canonical_desc: A young woman with short, curly red hair, wearing a dusty green jumpsuit and a worn-out utility belt, with a determined gaze.
characters.name: Elara

settings.canonical_desc: A small, enclosed biodome filled with vibrant plants, glowing under artificial lights, surrounded by the barren Martian landscape outside.
settings.name: Mars Garden

A.narration:
Under the shimmering red sky of Mars, among rows of emerald green, the last human gardener, Elara, knelt beside a new delivery. Dust clung to her boots as she opened the metallic crate marked "SEEDS". Her hands stopped at a packet, oddly labeled with names she’d never heard before.
Amara and Kai? Who are you supposed to be?
Elara glanced at the other packets, each bearing different names, none familiar. A chill ran through her despite the warmth of the artificial sun overhead.
Inside the control room, Elara connected with Mission Command. The walls hummed with the energy of unseen circuits, glowing softly with data streams.
Command, I received seeds with people’s names on them. They’re not in any registry.
Unknown seeds? Secure them immediately. Do not plant.
The line crackled and went silent. Elara stared at the blank screen, a decision forming in her mind.
Elara returned to the garden. The silence beneath the dome was profound, each plant a testament to human persistence. She picked up the packet again, the names whispering of untold futures.
No registry yet. Perhaps... not yet born.
Her resolve hardened. She dug into the soil, placing each seed with care, as if cradling potential life.
That night, Elara dreamt. The garden transformed around her, vibrant with new growth. Faces emerged from the foliage, children playing and laughing, their laughter echoing in her mind.
Thank you for planting us.
We’ll grow where you can’t see.
Elara awoke, the dream vivid, lingering like the taste of ripe berries.
Days turned to weeks. The seeds sprouted, unfamiliar plants reaching for the sky, vibrant and alive. The garden flourished beyond her wildest dreams, each plant a silent promise.
Maybe one day, your names will be known.
She stood among the greenery, now a tapestry woven with hope, where each leaf whispered stories of those yet to come. And as she tended the garden, she felt a part of a continuum, bridging her world and the worlds yet to be born.

characters.canonical_desc: A determined female gardener in her 30s, with short brown hair and a practical gardening outfit, embodying resilience and curiosity.
characters.name: Elara

characters.canonical_desc: An unseen authoritative voice, representing the control room, conveying urgency and concern.
characters.name: Mission Command

characters.canonical_desc: A joyful young girl, appearing around 8 years old, with long hair and a playful demeanor.
characters.name: Amara

characters.canonical_desc: A grateful young boy, appearing around 8 years old, with short hair and an adventurous spirit.
characters.name: Kai

settings.canonical_desc: A lush garden under a red Martian sky, filled with vibrant green plants and artificial sunlight.
settings.name: Mars Dome Garden

settings.canonical_desc: A high-tech room filled with screens and glowing data streams, buzzing with energy.
settings.name: Control Room

settings.canonical_desc: A surreal landscape filled with vibrant plants and children playing, embodying a dreamlike quality.
settings.name: Dreamscape
